title: Can you social engineer an AI? Plus: AI worms and the nonhuman identity problem
summary: IBM Security Intelligence podcast discusses whether AI systems are vulnerable to social engineering attacks similar to humans. The episode covers prompt injection attacks on Meta/Instagram, self-replicating AI worms, and nonhuman identity security gaps that are causing data breaches across organizations.
writeup: This IBM Technology podcast episode examines whether AI systems can be manipulated through social engineering tactics analogous to those used against humans, framing prompt injection and backdoor attacks as the mechanisms involved. It covers a self-replicating AI worm developed at the University of Toronto and prompt injection attacks targeting Meta and Instagram, illustrating how AI agents can be coerced or propagated against. It also draws on the Sophos State of Identity Security 2026 report, focusing on nonhuman identity breaches and credential management gaps.
